decisions.title_en: Qualcomm in talks to acquire Jim Keller's Tenstorrent for $8-10B
decisions.reason: story_key not seen in lookback. Reported Monday June 15 by The Information; Qualcomm in talks to buy AI chip startup Tenstorrent (founded by Jim Keller) at $8B-$10B to push into data-center/AI chips.

decisions.title_en: At Evian G7, leaders frame AI model concentration as systemic risk; Carney compares Fable 5 shutdown to 2008
decisions.reason: Previously published 2026-06-12 (CEOs attending) and 2026-06-15 (AI-sovereignty split). New info: with summit underway (day 1-2), leaders are debating AI infrastructure concentration as a financial-stability/systemic risk, with Canada's Carney explicitly comparing over-reliance on Mythos/Fable models to 2008 crisis structural vulnerabilities and pushing for diversification + common safety standards.

decisions.title_en: OpenAI launches $150M Partner Network to certify 300,000 consultants
decisions.reason: story_key not seen in lookback. Announced June 15: OpenAI's first formal partner program, $150M, target 300k certified consultants by year-end, launch cohort Accenture/Bain/BCG/McKinsey/PwC, three tiers, plus Forward Deployed Experts pilot.

decisions.title_en: Anthropic splits Agent SDK / claude -p usage into separate metered credit pool effective June 15
decisions.reason: story_key not seen in lookback. Effective June 15: Agent SDK, claude -p, Claude Code GitHub Actions and third-party agent usage move off subscription limits onto a separate monthly Agent SDK credit ($20 Pro/$100 Max5x/$200 Max20x) metered at full API rates, no rollover; heavy automation users face large effective cost increases.

decisions.title_en: Zuckerberg admits 'mistakes' in Meta AI restructuring, rules out more 2026 layoffs
decisions.reason: story_key not seen in lookback (distinct from meta-ai-cost-controls which is about token limits). June 12 internal memo: Zuckerberg concedes mistakes in AI reorg under Alexandr Wang's Superintelligence Labs, no more company-wide layoffs in 2026, restoring offsets/desks/hackathon and seeking new roles for model-training staff.

decisions.title_en: Shanghai's ACE Robotics open-sources Kairos world model, tops 4 embodied-AI benchmarks
decisions.reason: story_key not seen in lookback. June 15: ACE Robotics (Shanghai) says its open-source Kairos world model ranked #1 across RoboTwin 2.0, LIBERO-Plus, WorldModelBench Robot and DreamGen as of June 12; hybrid SSM + VLA backbone, real-time generative world model (Kairos 3.0-4B), available on GitHub/HF/ModelScope.

decisions.title_en: Altman, Amodei, Hassabis, Suleyman urge Congress to mandate synthetic DNA screening
decisions.reason: event_date 2026-06-03/05 is >5 days before 2026-06-16.

decisions.title_en: NVIDIA launches Ising, first open AI models for quantum computing
decisions.reason: Announced April 2026, far outside 5-day window.

decisions.title_en: Honor's Lightning robot runs Beijing half-marathon in 50:26
decisions.reason: Event April 19, 2026, outside window.

decisions.title_en: OpenAI acquires personal-finance startup Hiro
decisions.reason: Acquisition reported April 2026, outside window.

decisions.reason: Round closed May 27-28, 2026, outside 5-day window.

decisions.title_en: Novo Nordisk partners with OpenAI across operations

decisions.reason: Integration announced April 2026 (partnership at CES Jan 2026), outside window.

decisions.title_en: Microsoft $18B + OpenAI A$7B Australia AI infrastructure
decisions.reason: Microsoft deal April 2026, OpenAI deal Dec 2025; the 'June $18B deals' framing conflates older commitments. Outside window.

decisions.title_en: Google Gemma 4 open model (now on AWS Bedrock)
decisions.reason: Gemma 4 released April 2, 2026; June 15 is only Bedrock availability, a minor distribution update.

decisions.title_en: Gemini 3.5 Pro still pending (expected before June 30)
decisions.reason: Announced at I/O May 19; still not shipped as of June 16, so no new dateable development this week.

decisions.title_en: Fable 5 remains offline, day 5 of export-control shutdown
decisions.reason: Already published 2026-06-09 (Fable 5 launch) and 2026-06-13 (export-control shutdown). 'Still offline, license applications filed' is status continuation with no substantive new development.
